label.name: Dear student,We hope you are well.We would like to...
label.intro:
Dear student,

We hope you are well.

We would like to introduce you to the objectives of this self-study e-learning course that we have created for you:

  1. To offer you an additional original proposal to accompany your exchange experience. The purpose of the course is to help you validate and become aware of the softskills-related learning that you will certainly develop during your experience in an international context.
  1. Give you the opportunity to deepen:
  • on the development of competences linked to curiosity and openness to diversity in a multicultural context.
  • on the need for a global engagement (human rights, environment, equity and inclusion, etc.) and on the skills that come into play in the contrast with the exchange experience and the return to everyday life.
  • on the development of skills linked to adaptability and openness to new learning offered by contact with people from different backgrounds. As well as the need to carry out day-to-day tasks, both at and outside the university, and in general in living independently in this context.
  • on the opportunity to develop the problem-solving and decision-making skills throughout the exchange process (before, during and after), addressing issues such as digitisation and linguistic competence.

This proposal is open and voluntary and of course, you are welcome to develop it as you wish:

  • Individually or together with other students who are participating in an exchange experience.
  • In the format that suits you best (notebook, corkboard, A2, podcast, mini-videos...).

Customise your learning pathway, map your experience, adapting it to your personal characteristics and focusing on the development of a professional profile enriched by the exchange experience, beyond the academic aspects.

We encourage you to share the final result of your mapping experience with the rest of the participants. 

If you feel like it, you can also share your mapping with the ORI. We are very interested in knowing the details of your exchange experience in order to better guide other exchange students in the future.

Thank you very much in advance for your participation.

Have a nice day, take care.

 

Kind regards, 

IRO Team
